title: Federated Inference: Toward Privacy-Preserving Collaborative and Incentivized Model Serving
summary: This paper proposes Federated Inference as a collaborative paradigm where independently trained models work together at inference time without sharing data or parameters. The research identifies key design dimensions and trade-offs between privacy preservation, performance gains, and non-IID data, revealing system-level behaviors distinct from federated learning and traditional ensembles.
content:
arXiv:2603.02214v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Federated Inference (FI) studies how independently trained and privately owned models can collaborate at inference time without sharing data or model parameters. While recent work has explored secure and distributed inference from disparate perspectives, a unified abstraction and system-level understanding of FI remain lacking. This paper positions FI as a distinct collaborative paradigm, complementary to federated learning, and identifies two fundamental requirements that govern its feasibility: inference-time privacy preservation and meaningful performance gains through collaboration. We formalize FI as a protected collaborative computation, analyze its core design dimensions, and examine the structural trade-offs that arise when privacy constraints, non-IID data, and limited observability are jointly imposed at inference time. Through a concrete instantiation and empirical analysis, we highlight recurring friction points in privacy-preserving inference, ensemble-based collaboration, and incentive alignment. Our findings suggest that FI exhibits system-level behaviors that cannot be directly inherited from training-time federation or classical ensemble methods. Overall, this work provides a unifying perspective on FI and outlines open challenges that must be addressed to enable practical, scalable, and privacy-preserving collaborative inference systems.

zh.title: 联邦推理：面向隐私保护的协作和激励模型服务
zh.summary: 本文提出联邦推理作为一种协作范式，其中独立训练的模型在推理时协同工作，无需共享数据或参数。该研究确定了隐私保护、性能收益和非独立同分布数据之间的关键设计维度和权衡，揭示了与联邦学习和传统集成不同的系统级行为。

fr.title: Inférence fédérée : vers un service de modèle collaboratif et incité préservant la confidentialité
fr.summary: Cet article propose l'inférence fédérée comme un paradigme collaboratif où des modèles formés indépendamment travaillent ensemble au moment de l'inférence sans partager les données ou les paramètres. La recherche identifie les dimensions de conception clés et les compromis entre la préservation de la confidentialité, les gains de performance et les données non-IID, révélant des comportements au niveau du système distincts de l'apprentissage fédéré et des ensembles traditionnels.

de.title: Föderierte Inferenz: zum datenschutzschonenden kollaborativen und anreizgesteuerten Modellbereitstellung
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt föderierte Inferenz als kollaboratives Paradigma vor, bei dem unabhängig trainierte Modelle zur Inferenzzeit zusammenarbeiten, ohne Daten oder Parameter auszutauschen. Die Forschung identifiziert Schlüssel-Designdimensionen und Kompromisse zwischen Datenschutzschutz, Leistungsgewinnen und nicht-IID-Daten und offenbart Verhaltensweisen auf Systemebene, die sich von föderiertem Lernen und traditionellen Ensembles unterscheiden.

es.title: Inferencia federada: hacia el servicio de modelo colaborativo e incentivado que preserva la privacidad
es.summary: Este documento propone la inferencia federada como un paradigma colaborativo donde modelos entrenados independientemente trabajan juntos en el momento de la inferencia sin compartir datos ni parámetros. La investigación identifica dimensiones de diseño clave y compromisos entre preservación de privacidad, ganancias de rendimiento y datos no-IID, revelando comportamientos a nivel de sistema distintos del aprendizaje federado y los conjuntos tradicionales.

title: Engineering Reasoning and Instruction (ERI) Benchmark: A Large Taxonomy-driven Dataset for Foundation Models and Agents
summary: The Engineering Reasoning and Instruction (ERI) benchmark provides a comprehensive dataset of 57,750 engineering problems across nine fields and seven intent types for training and evaluating specialized LLMs and agents. Evaluation of frontier models shows a three-tier performance structure, with GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4, and DeepSeek V3.1 significantly outperforming smaller models on graduate-level problems.
content:
arXiv:2603.02239v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The Engineering Reasoning and Instruction (ERI) benchmark is a taxonomy-driven instruction dataset designed to train and evaluate engineering-capable large language models (LLMs) and agents. This dataset spans nine engineering fields (namely: civil, mechanical, electrical, chemical, environmental, aerospace, materials, fire, and industrial engineering) and 55 subdomains, and is crossed with seven intent types (i.e., definition, explanation, calculation, comparison, design/synthesis, troubleshooting, and code-related) and three difficulty tiers (undergraduate, graduate, and professional), yielding 57,750 records with field/subdomain/type/difficulty metadata and solution formatting. We examined ERI via seven LLMs and report a statistically significant three-tier performance structure, with frontier models (GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4, DeepSeek V3.1) achieving mean scores above 4.30 on a five-point scale, while mid-tier and smaller models exhibited progressively higher failure rates and steeper performance degradation on graduate-level questions. To address circularity concerns inherent in LLM benchmarks, we developed a convergent validation protocol that leverages cross-provider independence, multi-judge averaging, and frontier-model agreement analysis to empirically bound hallucination risk to 1.7%. ERI is released with taxonomy specifications, validation scripts, and an evaluation harness to enable reproducible comparisons and regression testing for instruction tuning, routing, retrieval-augmented evaluation, and agentic tool-use workflows in engineering settings.

zh.title: 工程推理和指令（ERI）基准：用于基础模型和代理的大规模分类法驱动数据集
zh.summary: 工程推理和指令（ERI）基准提供了一个包含57,750个工程问题的综合数据集，跨越九个领域和七种意图类型，用于训练和评估专门的大语言模型和代理。对前沿模型的评估显示了三层性能结构，GPT-5、Claude Sonnet 4和DeepSeek V3.1在研究生级问题上明显优于较小的模型。

fr.title: Benchmark d'ingénierie du raisonnement et des instructions (ERI) : un grand ensemble de données basé sur une taxonomie pour les modèles fondamentaux et les agents
fr.summary: Le benchmark ERI fournit un ensemble de données complet de 57 750 problèmes d'ingénierie couvrant neuf domaines et sept types d'intentions pour former et évaluer les LLM et les agents spécialisés. L'évaluation des modèles de pointe montre une structure de performance à trois niveaux, avec GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4 et DeepSeek V3.1 surpassant nettement les modèles plus petits sur les problèmes de niveau universitaire.

de.title: Engineering Reasoning and Instruction (ERI) Benchmark: Ein großer taxonomiegetriebener Datensatz für Grundmodelle und Agenten
de.summary: Der Engineering Reasoning and Instruction (ERI) Benchmark bietet einen umfassenden Datensatz von 57.750 Engineeringproblemen über neun Felder und sieben Intentionstypen zum Trainieren und Bewerten spezialisierter LLMs und Agenten. Die Bewertung von Frontier-Modellen zeigt eine dreistufige Leistungsstruktur, wobei GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4 und DeepSeek V3.1 kleinere Modelle bei Graduiertenstufenproblemen deutlich überperformen.

es.title: Benchmark de razonamiento e instrucción de ingeniería (ERI): un conjunto de datos grande impulsado por taxonomía para modelos fundamentales y agentes
es.summary: El benchmark ERI proporciona un conjunto de datos completo de 57.750 problemas de ingeniería en nueve campos y siete tipos de intención para entrenar y evaluar LLM y agentes especializados. La evaluación de modelos de vanguardia muestra una estructura de rendimiento de tres niveles, con GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4 y DeepSeek V3.1 superando significativamente a los modelos más pequeños en problemas a nivel de posgrado.

title: SuperLocalMemory: Privacy-Preserving Multi-Agent Memory with Bayesian Trust Defense Against Memory Poisoning
summary: SuperLocalMemory introduces a local-first memory system for multi-agent AI that defends against memory poisoning attacks using architectural isolation and Bayesian trust scoring, while maintaining privacy without cloud dependencies. The system combines SQLite storage with adaptive learning-to-rank and achieves 10.6ms median search latency with strong defenses against adversarial attacks.
content:
arXiv:2603.02240v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present SuperLocalMemory, a local-first memory system for multi-agent AI that defends against OWASP ASI06 memory poisoning through architectural isolation and Bayesian trust scoring, while personalizing retrieval through adaptive learning-to-rank -- all without cloud dependencies or LLM inference calls. As AI agents increasingly rely on persistent memory, cloud-based memory systems create centralized attack surfaces where poisoned memories propagate across sessions and users -- a threat demonstrated in documented attacks against production systems. Our architecture combines SQLite-backed storage with FTS5 full-text search, Leiden-based knowledge graph clustering, an event-driven coordination layer with per-agent provenance, and an adaptive re-ranking framework that learns user preferences through three-layer behavioral analysis (cross-project technology preferences, project context detection, and workflow pattern mining). Evaluation across seven benchmark dimensions demonstrates 10.6ms median search latency, zero concurrency errors under 10 simultaneous agents, trust separation (gap =0.90) with 72% trust degradation for sleeper attacks, and 104% improvement in NDCG@5 when adaptive re-ranking is enabled. Behavioral data is isolated in a separate database with GDPR Article 17 erasure support. SuperLocalMemory is open-source (MIT) and integrates with 17+ development tools via Model Context Protocol.

zh.title: SuperLocalMemory：具有贝叶斯信任防御的隐私保护多代理记忆系统
zh.summary: SuperLocalMemory引入了一个针对多代理AI的本地优先内存系统，使用架构隔离和贝叶斯信任评分来防御内存中毒攻击，同时在没有云依赖的情况下保持隐私。该系统将SQLite存储与自适应学习排序相结合，实现了10.6毫秒的中位数搜索延迟，对对抗攻击具有强有力的防御。

fr.title: SuperLocalMemory : mémoire multi-agents préservant la confidentialité avec défense de confiance bayésienne contre l'empoisonnement de la mémoire
fr.summary: SuperLocalMemory introduit un système de mémoire local d'abord pour l'IA multi-agents qui se défend contre les attaques d'empoisonnement de mémoire en utilisant l'isolement architectural et la notation de confiance bayésienne, tout en maintenant la confidentialité sans dépendances cloud. Le système combine le stockage SQLite avec un classement adaptatif et atteint une latence de recherche médiane de 10,6 ms avec des défenses solides contre les attaques adversariales.

de.title: SuperLocalMemory: Datenschutzschonender Multi-Agent-Speicher mit bayesischer Vertrauensverteidigung gegen Speichervergiftung
de.summary: SuperLocalMemory führt ein lokal-erstes Speichersystem für Multi-Agent-KI ein, das sich gegen Speichervergiftungsangriffe durch architektonische Isolation und bayesische Vertrauensbewertung wehrt, während die Datenschutzbestimmungen ohne Cloud-Abhängigkeiten eingehalten werden. Das System kombiniert SQLite-Speicher mit adaptivem Learning-to-Rank und erreicht eine mittlere Suchlatenz von 10,6 ms mit starken Abwehrmaßnahmen gegen adversarische Angriffe.

es.title: SuperLocalMemory: Memoria multi-agente que preserva la privacidad con defensa de confianza bayesiana contra el envenenamiento de memoria
es.summary: SuperLocalMemory introduce un sistema de memoria local-first para IA multi-agente que se defiende contra ataques de envenenamiento de memoria usando aislamiento arquitectónico y puntuación de confianza bayesiana, mientras mantiene privacidad sin dependencias en la nube. El sistema combina almacenamiento SQLite con ranking adaptativo que aprende y logra una latencia de búsqueda mediana de 10.6ms con defensas sólidas contra ataques adversariales.

title: Estimating Visual Attribute Effects in Advertising from Observational Data: A Deepfake-Informed Double Machine Learning Approach
summary: This paper develops DICE-DML, a framework using deepfake-generated images to isolate causal effects of visual attributes in digital advertising where standard methods fail. Applied to Instagram influencer data, the method finds a statistically valid but marginal negative effect of darker skin tone on engagement, demonstrating robust causal inference for visual content.
content:
arXiv:2603.02359v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Digital advertising increasingly relies on visual content, yet marketers lack rigorous methods for understanding how specific visual attributes causally affect consumer engagement. This paper addresses a fundamental methodological challenge: estimating causal effects when the treatment, such as a model's skin tone, is an attribute embedded within the image itself. Standard approaches like Double Machine Learning (DML) fail in this setting because vision encoders entangle treatment information with confounding variables, producing severely biased estimates. We develop DICE-DML (Deepfake-Informed Control Encoder for Double Machine Learning), a framework that leverages generative AI to disentangle treatment from confounders. The approach combines three mechanisms: (1) deepfake-generated image pairs that isolate treatment variation; (2) DICE-Diff adversarial learning on paired difference vectors, where background signals cancel to reveal pure treatment fingerprints; and (3) orthogonal projection that geometrically removes treatment-axis components. In simulations with known ground truth, DICE-DML reduces root mean squared error by 73-97% compared to standard DML, with the strongest improvement (97.5%) at the null effect point, demonstrating robust Type I error control. Applying DICE-DML to 232,089 Instagram influencer posts, we estimate the causal effect of skin tone on engagement. Standard DML produces diagnostically invalid results (negative outcome R^2), while DICE-DML achieves valid confounding control (R^2 = 0.63) and estimates a marginally significant negative effect of darker skin tone (-522 likes; p = 0.062), substantially smaller than the biased standard estimate. Our framework provides a principled approach for causal inference with visual data when treatments and confounders coexist within images.

zh.title: 从观察数据估计广告中的视觉属性效应：一种深度伪造启发的双重机器学习方法
zh.summary: 该论文开发了DICE-DML框架，使用深度伪造生成的图像来隔离数字广告中视觉属性的因果效应，其中标准方法失败。应用于Instagram影响者数据，该方法发现较深肤色对参与度的统计有效但边际负面影响，展示了视觉内容的稳健因果推断。

fr.title: Estimation des effets des attributs visuels dans la publicité à partir de données observationnelles : une approche d'apprentissage automatique double informée par la technologie deepfake
fr.summary: Cet article développe DICE-DML, un cadre utilisant des images générées par deepfake pour isoler les effets causals des attributs visuels dans la publicité numérique où les méthodes standard échouent. Appliqué aux données des influenceurs Instagram, la méthode trouve un effet négatif statistiquement valide mais marginal du teint plus foncé sur l'engagement, démontrant une inférence causale robuste pour le contenu visuel.

de.title: Schätzung visueller Attributeffekte in der Werbung aus Beobachtungsdaten: Ein deepfake-inspirierter Ansatz des doppelten maschinellen Lernens
de.summary: Dieses Papier entwickelt DICE-DML, ein Framework, das deepfake-generierte Bilder verwendet, um kausale Effekte visueller Attribute in der digitalen Werbung zu isolieren, wo standardmäßige Methoden fehlschlagen. Angewendet auf Instagram-Influencer-Daten findet die Methode einen statistisch gültigen, aber marginalen negativen Effekt dunklerer Hautfarbe auf das Engagement und demonstriert robuste kausale Inferenz für visuellen Inhalt.

es.title: Estimación de los efectos de atributos visuales en la publicidad a partir de datos observacionales: un enfoque de aprendizaje automático doble informado por deepfake
es.summary: Este artículo desarrolla DICE-DML, un marco que utiliza imágenes generadas por deepfake para aislar los efectos causales de los atributos visuales en la publicidad digital donde los métodos estándar fallan. Aplicado a datos de influencers de Instagram, el método encuentra un efecto negativo estadísticamente válido pero marginal del tono de piel más oscuro en la participación, demostrando una inferencia causal robusta para contenido visual.

title: Can machines be uncertain?
summary: The paper examines how AI systems can realize states of uncertainty from functionalist and behavioral perspectives across symbolic, connectionist, and hybrid architectures. It distinguishes between epistemic uncertainty in data and subjective uncertainty in the system, proposing that some uncertainty states constitute interrogative attitudes rather than propositions.
content:
arXiv:2603.02365v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The paper investigates whether and how AI systems can realize states of uncertainty. By adopting a functionalist and behavioral perspective, it examines how symbolic, connectionist and hybrid architectures make room for uncertainty. The paper distinguishes between epistemic uncertainty, or uncertainty inherent in the data or information, and subjective uncertainty, or the system's own attitude of being uncertainty. It further distinguishes between distributed and discrete realizations of subjective uncertainty. A key contribution is the idea that some states of uncertainty are interrogative attitudes whose content is a question rather than a proposition.

zh.title: 机器能否具有不确定性？
zh.summary: 该论文从功能主义和行为主义视角研究AI系统如何在符号、连接主义和混合架构中实现不确定性状态。它区分了数据中的认识论不确定性和系统中的主观不确定性，提出某些不确定性状态构成疑问态度而非命题。

fr.title: Les machines peuvent-elles être incertaines ?
fr.summary: Cet article examine comment les systèmes d'IA peuvent réaliser des états d'incertitude selon les perspectives fonctionnalistes et comportementales dans les architectures symboliques, connexionnistes et hybrides. Il distingue l'incertitude épistémique dans les données de l'incertitude subjective dans le système, proposant que certains états d'incertitude constituent des attitudes interrogatives plutôt que des propositions.

de.title: Können Maschinen unsicher sein?
de.summary: Das Papier untersucht, wie KI-Systeme Unsicherheitszustände aus funktionalistischer und verhaltensbasierter Perspektive in symbolischen, konnektionistischen und hybriden Architekturen realisieren können. Es unterscheidet zwischen epistemischer Unsicherheit in Daten und subjektiver Unsicherheit im System und schlägt vor, dass einige Unsicherheitszustände interrogative Einstellungen darstellen, anstatt Aussagen zu sein.

es.title: ¿Pueden las máquinas ser inciertas?
es.summary: El artículo examina cómo los sistemas de IA pueden realizar estados de incertidumbre desde perspectivas funcionalistas y de comportamiento en arquitecturas simbólicas, conexionistas e híbridas. Distingue entre la incertidumbre epistémica en los datos y la incertidumbre subjetiva en el sistema, proponiendo que algunos estados de incertidumbre constituyen actitudes interrogativas en lugar de proposiciones.

title: COOL-MC: Verifying and Explaining RL Policies for Platelet Inventory Management
summary: COOL-MC applies reinforcement learning with probabilistic model checking to verify and explain an RL policy for blood bank platelet inventory management, a critical supply chain problem. Results show the trained policy achieves 2.9% stockout probability and 1.1% wastage probability while primarily relying on inventory age distribution decisions.
content:
arXiv:2603.02396v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Platelets expire within five days. Blood banks face uncertain daily demand and must balance ordering decisions between costly wastage from overstocking and life-threatening shortages from understocking. Reinforcement learning (RL) can learn effective ordering policies for this Markov decision process (MDP), but the resulting neural policies remain black boxes, hindering trust and adoption in safety-critical domains. We apply COOL-MC, a tool that combines RL with probabilistic model checking and explainable RL, to verify and explain a trained policy for the MDP on platelet inventory management inspired by Haijema et al. By constructing a policy-induced discrete-time Markov chain (which includes only the reachable states under the trained policy to reduce memory usage), we verify PCTL properties and provide feature-level explanations. Results show that the trained policy achieves a 2.9% stockout probability and a 1.1% inventory-full (potential wastage) probability within a 200-step horizon, primarily attends to the age distribution of inventory rather than other features such as day of week or pending orders. Action reachability analysis reveals that the policy employs a diverse replenishment strategy, with most order quantities reached quickly, while several are never selected. Counterfactual analysis shows that replacing medium-large orders with smaller ones leaves both safety probabilities nearly unchanged, indicating that these orders are placed in well-buffered inventory states. This first formal verification and explanation of an RL platelet inventory management policy demonstrates COOL-MC's value for transparent, auditable decision-making in safety-critical healthcare supply chain domains.

zh.title: COOL-MC: 血小板库存管理强化学习策略的验证和解释
zh.summary: COOL-MC应用强化学习和概率模型检查来验证和解释血库血小板库存管理的强化学习策略，这是一个关键的供应链问题。结果显示训练的策略实现了2.9%的缺货概率和1.1%的浪费概率，同时主要依靠库存年龄分布决策。

fr.title: COOL-MC : Vérification et explication des politiques d'apprentissage par renforcement pour la gestion des stocks de plaquettes
fr.summary: COOL-MC applique l'apprentissage par renforcement avec vérification de modèle probabiliste pour vérifier et expliquer une politique d'apprentissage par renforcement pour la gestion des stocks de plaquettes dans les banques de sang, un problème critique de la chaîne d'approvisionnement. Les résultats montrent que la politique entraînée atteint une probabilité de rupture de stock de 2,9 % et une probabilité de gaspillage de 1,1 % tout en s'appuyant principalement sur des décisions de distribution d'âge des stocks.

de.title: COOL-MC: Verifikation und Erklärung von RL-Richtlinien für die Verwaltung des Thrombozytenbestands
de.summary: COOL-MC wendet Verstärkungslernens mit probabilistischer Modellprüfung an, um eine RL-Richtlinie für die Verwaltung des Thrombozytenbestands in Blutbanken, ein kritisches Lieferkettenproblem, zu verifizieren und zu erklären. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass die trainierte Richtlinie eine Fehlbestandwahrscheinlichkeit von 2,9 % und eine Verschwendungswahrscheinlichkeit von 1,1 % erreicht, während sie sich hauptsächlich auf Entscheidungen zur Bestandsaltersverteilung verlässt.

es.title: COOL-MC: Verificación y explicación de políticas de aprendizaje por refuerzo para la gestión del inventario de plaquetas
es.summary: COOL-MC aplica aprendizaje por refuerzo con verificación de modelos probabilísticos para verificar y explicar una política de aprendizaje por refuerzo para la gestión del inventario de plaquetas en bancos de sangre, un problema crítico de la cadena de suministro. Los resultados muestran que la política entrenada logra una probabilidad de desabastecimiento del 2,9 % y una probabilidad de desperdicio del 1,1 % mientras depende principalmente de decisiones de distribución de antigüedad del inventario.

title: VL-KGE: Vision-Language Models Meet Knowledge Graph Embeddings
summary: Vision-Language Knowledge Graph Embeddings (VL-KGE) integrates vision-language models with knowledge graph embedding methods to learn unified multimodal representations of entities and relations. Testing on WikiArt and other multimodal knowledge graphs demonstrates consistent improvements over unimodal and traditional multimodal baselines in link prediction tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.02435v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world multimodal knowledge graphs (MKGs) are inherently heterogeneous, modeling entities that are associated with diverse modalities. Traditional knowledge graph embedding (KGE) methods excel at learning continuous representations of entities and relations, yet they are typically designed for unimodal settings. Recent approaches extend KGE to multimodal settings but remain constrained, often processing modalities in isolation, resulting in weak cross-modal alignment, and relying on simplistic assumptions such as uniform modality availability across entities. Vision-Language Models (VLMs) offer a powerful way to align diverse modalities within a shared embedding space. We propose Vision-Language Knowledge Graph Embeddings (VL-KGE), a framework that integrates cross-modal alignment from VLMs with structured relational modeling to learn unified multimodal representations of knowledge graphs. Experiments on WN9-IMG and two novel fine art MKGs, WikiArt-MKG-v1 and WikiArt-MKG-v2, demonstrate that VL-KGE consistently improves over traditional unimodal and multimodal KGE methods in link prediction tasks. Our results highlight the value of VLMs for multimodal KGE, enabling more robust and structured reasoning over large-scale heterogeneous knowledge graphs.

zh.title: VL-KGE：视觉语言模型与知识图谱嵌入的融合
zh.summary: 视觉语言知识图谱嵌入（VL-KGE）将视觉语言模型与知识图谱嵌入方法相结合，以学习实体和关系的统一多模态表示。在WikiArt和其他多模态知识图谱上的测试表明，在链接预测任务中相比单模态和传统多模态基线方法获得了一致的改进。

fr.title: VL-KGE : Les modèles vision-langage rencontrent les embeddings de graphes de connaissances
fr.summary: Vision-Language Knowledge Graph Embeddings (VL-KGE) intègre les modèles vision-langage avec les méthodes d'embedding de graphes de connaissances pour apprendre des représentations multimodales unifiées des entités et des relations. Les tests sur WikiArt et d'autres graphes de connaissances multimodaux démontrent des améliorations constantes par rapport aux baselines unimodales et multimodales traditionnelles dans les tâches de prédiction de liens.

de.title: VL-KGE: Vision-Language-Modelle treffen auf Knowledge-Graph-Embeddings
de.summary: Vision-Language Knowledge Graph Embeddings (VL-KGE) integriert Vision-Language-Modelle mit Knowledge-Graph-Embedding-Methoden, um einheitliche multimodale Darstellungen von Entitäten und Relationen zu erlernen. Tests auf WikiArt und anderen multimodalen Wissensgraphen zeigen konsistente Verbesserungen gegenüber unimodalen und traditionellen multimodalen Baselines bei Link-Prediction-Aufgaben.

es.title: VL-KGE: Modelos de Visión-Lenguaje se Encuentran con Embeddings de Grafos de Conocimiento
es.summary: Vision-Language Knowledge Graph Embeddings (VL-KGE) integra modelos de visión-lenguaje con métodos de embedding de grafos de conocimiento para aprender representaciones multimodales unificadas de entidades y relaciones. Las pruebas en WikiArt y otros grafos de conocimiento multimodales demuestran mejoras consistentes sobre baselines unimodales y multimodales tradicionales en tareas de predicción de enlaces.

title: Diagnosing Retrieval vs. Utilization Bottlenecks in LLM Agent Memory
summary: This paper introduces a diagnostic framework for analyzing LLM agent memory systems, comparing three write strategies with three retrieval methods on real-world tasks. The findings reveal that retrieval method is the dominant factor in performance, with raw chunking matching or outperforming expensive lossy alternatives, suggesting current memory pipelines may discard useful context.
content:
arXiv:2603.02473v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Memory-augmented LLM agents store and retrieve information from prior interactions, yet the relative importance of how memories are written versus how they are retrieved remains unclear. We introduce a diagnostic framework that analyzes how performance differences manifest across write strategies, retrieval methods, and memory utilization behavior, and apply it to a 3x3 study crossing three write strategies (raw chunks, Mem0-style fact extraction, MemGPT-style summarization) with three retrieval methods (cosine, BM25, hybrid reranking). On LoCoMo, retrieval method is the dominant factor: average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%) but only 3-8 points across write strategies. Raw chunked storage, which requires zero LLM calls, matches or outperforms expensive lossy alternatives, suggesting that current memory pipelines may discard useful context that downstream retrieval mechanisms fail to compensate for. Failure analysis shows that performance breakdowns most often manifest at the retrieval stage rather than at utilization. We argue that, under current retrieval practices, improving retrieval quality yields larger gains than increasing write-time sophistication. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/boqiny/memory-probe.

zh.title: 诊断LLM代理记忆中的检索与利用瓶颈
zh.summary: 本文介绍了一个诊断框架，用于分析LLM代理记忆系统，在真实任务上比较了三种写入策略和三种检索方法。研究发现检索方法是性能的主要影响因素，原始分块与昂贵的有损替代方案相匹配或优于它们，这表明当前记忆管道可能会丢弃有用的上下文。

fr.title: Diagnostic des goulots d'étranglement de récupération versus utilisation dans la mémoire des agents LLM
fr.summary: Cet article introduit un cadre diagnostic pour analyser les systèmes de mémoire des agents LLM, comparant trois stratégies d'écriture avec trois méthodes de récupération sur des tâches du monde réel. Les résultats montrent que la méthode de récupération est le facteur dominant pour la performance, avec le chunking brut correspondant à ou surpassant les alternatives coûteuses et avec perte, ce qui suggère que les pipelines de mémoire actuels peuvent rejeter du contexte utile.

de.title: Diagnose von Abruf- versus Nutzungsengpässen im LLM-Agenten-Speicher
de.summary: Dieses Papier stellt ein diagnostisches Framework zur Analyse von LLM-Agenten-Speichersystemen vor und vergleicht drei Schreibstrategien mit drei Abrufmethoden auf realen Aufgaben. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass die Abrufmethode der dominante Faktor für die Leistung ist, wobei Raw Chunking teure verlustbehaftete Alternativen erreicht oder übertrifft, was darauf hindeutet, dass aktuelle Speicher-Pipelines möglicherweise nützliche Kontexte verwerfen.

es.title: Diagnóstico de Cuellos de Botella de Recuperación versus Utilización en la Memoria del Agente LLM
es.summary: Este documento introduce un marco de diagnóstico para analizar sistemas de memoria de agentes LLM, comparando tres estrategias de escritura con tres métodos de recuperación en tareas del mundo real. Los hallazgos revelan que el método de recuperación es el factor dominante en el rendimiento, con chunking sin procesar igualando o superando alternativas costosas con pérdida, lo que sugiere que los pipelines de memoria actuales pueden descartar contexto útil.

title: PRISM: Pushing the Frontier of Deep Think via Process Reward Model-Guided Inference
summary: PRISM is a process reward model-guided inference algorithm that improves deep reasoning by treating candidate solutions as particles in an energy landscape and reshaping populations through score-guided resampling. Achieving 90.0% accuracy on AIME25 and 75.4% on HMMT25, PRISM matches or exceeds much larger models while providing consistent directional correction during refinement.
content:
arXiv:2603.02479v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: DEEPTHINK methods improve reasoning by generating, refining, and aggregating populations of candidate solutions, which enables strong performance on complex mathematical and scientific tasks. However, existing frameworks often lack reliable correctness signals during inference, which creates a population-enhancement bottleneck where deeper deliberation amplifies errors, suppresses correct minority solutions, and yields weak returns to additional compute. In this paper, we introduce a functional decomposition of DEEPTHINK systems and propose PRISM, a Process Reward Model (PRM)-guided inference algorithm that uses step-level verification to guide both population refinement and solution aggregation. During refinement, PRISM treats candidate solutions as particles in a PRM-defined energy landscape and reshapes the population through score-guided resampling and stochastic refinement, which concentrates probability mass on higher-quality reasoning while preserving diversity. Across mathematics and science benchmarks, PRISM is competitive with or outperforms existing DEEPTHINK methods, reaching 90.0%, 75.4%, and 71.4% with gpt-oss-20b on AIME25, HMMT25, and GPQA Diamond, respectively, while matching or exceeding gpt-oss-120b. Additionally, our analysis shows that PRISM produces consistent net-directional correction during refinement, remains reliable when the initial population contains few correct candidates, and often lies on the compute-accuracy Pareto frontier.

zh.title: PRISM：通过过程奖励模型引导推理推进深层思考的前沿
zh.summary: PRISM是一种过程奖励模型引导的推理算法，通过将候选解视为能量景观中的粒子，并通过分数引导的重新采样来重塑种群，从而改进深层推理。在AIME25上达到90.0%的准确率，在HMMT25上达到75.4%，PRISM与更大的模型相匹配或超过它们，同时在优化过程中提供一致的方向性纠正。

fr.title: PRISM : Repousser les frontières de la pensée profonde via l'inférence guidée par le modèle de récompense de processus
fr.summary: PRISM est un algorithme d'inférence guidé par un modèle de récompense de processus qui améliore le raisonnement profond en traitant les solutions candidates comme des particules dans un paysage énergétique et en remodeling les populations par rééchantillonnage guidé par les scores. Obtenant une précision de 90,0% sur AIME25 et 75,4% sur HMMT25, PRISM égale ou dépasse des modèles beaucoup plus grands tout en fournissant une correction directionnelle constante lors du raffinement.

de.title: PRISM: Die Grenzen des tiefen Denkens durch prozessbasierte Reward-Modell-gesteuerte Inferenz verschieben
de.summary: PRISM ist ein prozessbasiertes Reward-Modell-gesteuerter Inferenzalgorithmus, der tiefes Reasoning verbessert, indem er Kandidatenlösungen als Partikel in einer Energielandschaft behandelt und Populationen durch score-gesteuerte Resampling umgestaltet. Mit einer Genauigkeit von 90,0% auf AIME25 und 75,4% auf HMMT25 erreicht oder übertrifft PRISM viel größere Modelle, während es während der Verfeinerung eine konsistente Richtungskorrektur bietet.

es.title: PRISM: Empujando la Frontera del Pensamiento Profundo mediante Inferencia Guiada por Modelo de Recompensa de Proceso
es.summary: PRISM es un algoritmo de inferencia guiado por un modelo de recompensa de proceso que mejora el razonamiento profundo al tratar soluciones candidatas como partículas en un paisaje energético y remodelar poblaciones mediante remuestreo guiado por puntuación. Logrando una precisión del 90,0% en AIME25 y 75,4% en HMMT25, PRISM iguala o supera modelos mucho más grandes mientras proporciona una corrección direccional consistente durante el refinamiento.

title: Revealing Positive and Negative Role Models to Help People Make Good Decisions
summary: The paper studies how a social planner can maximize social welfare by strategically revealing whether network role models are positive or negative under a limited disclosure budget. Despite the technical challenge that negative labels break submodularity, the authors achieve constant-factor approximations to optimal welfare gains using a proxy welfare function.
content:
arXiv:2603.02495v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We consider a setting where agents take action by following their role models in a social network, and study strategies for a social planner to help agents by revealing whether the role models are positive or negative. Specifically, agents observe a local neighborhood of possible role models they can emulate, but do not know their true labels. Revealing a positive label encourages emulation, while revealing a negative one redirects agents toward alternative options. The social planner observes all labels, but operates under a limited disclosure budget that it selectively allocates to maximize social welfare (the expected number of agents who emulate adjacent positive role models). We consider both algorithms and hardness results for welfare maximization, and provide a sample-complexity guarantee when the planner observes a sampled subset of agents. We also consider fairness guarantees when agents belong to different groups. It is a technical challenge that the ability to reveal negative role models breaks submodularity. We thus introduce a proxy welfare function that remains submodular even when revealed targets include negative ones. When each agent has at most a constant number of negative target neighbors, we use this proxy to achieve a constant-factor approximation to the true optimal welfare gain. When agents belong to different groups, we also show that each group's welfare gain is within a constant factor of the optimum achievable if the full budget were allocated to that group. Beyond this basic model, we also propose an intervention model that directly connects high-risk agents to positive role models, and a coverage radius model that expands the visibility of selected positive role models. Lastly, we conduct extensive experiments on four real-world datasets to support our theoretical results and assess the effectiveness of the proposed algorithms.

zh.title: 揭示正面和负面榜样以帮助人们做出正确决定
zh.summary: 该论文研究了社会规划者如何在有限的披露预算下，通过战略性地揭示网络中角色模范是正面还是负面来最大化社会福利。尽管负面标签破坏了子模块性的技术挑战，作者通过使用代理福利函数实现了最优福利增益的常数因子近似。

fr.title: Révéler les modèles de rôles positifs et négatifs pour aider les gens à prendre de bonnes décisions
fr.summary: L'article étudie comment un planificateur social peut maximiser le bien-être social en révélant stratégiquement si les modèles de rôles du réseau sont positifs ou négatifs sous un budget de divulgation limité. Malgré le défi technique que les étiquettes négatives brisent la sous-modularité, les auteurs réalisent des approximations à facteur constant aux gains de bien-être optimaux en utilisant une fonction de bien-être proxy.

de.title: Offenlegung von positiven und negativen Vorbildern, um Menschen bei guten Entscheidungen zu helfen
de.summary: Die Arbeit untersucht, wie ein Sozialplaner das soziale Wohlergehen maximieren kann, indem er strategisch offenlegt, ob Netzwerk-Vorbilder positiv oder negativ sind, unter einem begrenzten Offenlegungsbudget. Trotz der technischen Herausforderung, dass negative Etiketten Submodularität brechen, erzielen die Autoren konstante Approximationen zu optimalen Wohlfahrtsgewinnen unter Verwendung einer Proxy-Wohlfahrtsfunktion.

es.title: Revelar modelos de rol positivos y negativos para ayudar a las personas a tomar buenas decisiones
es.summary: El artículo estudia cómo un planificador social puede maximizar el bienestar social revelando estratégicamente si los modelos de rol de la red son positivos o negativos bajo un presupuesto de divulgación limitado. A pesar del desafío técnico de que las etiquetas negativas rompan la submodularidad, los autores logran aproximaciones de factor constante a las ganancias de bienestar óptimas utilizando una función de bienestar proxy.

title: NeuroProlog: Multi-Task Fine-Tuning for Neurosymbolic Mathematical Reasoning via the Cocktail Effect
summary: NeuroProlog combines neurosymbolic approaches with LLMs by compiling math word problems into executable Prolog programs with formal verification guarantees. A multi-task cocktail training strategy jointly optimizes formula translation, program synthesis, and answer alignment, achieving accuracy improvements of 3.4%-5.5% over baselines across multiple model sizes.
content:
arXiv:2603.02504v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance on natural language tasks but remain unreliable in mathematical reasoning, frequently generating fluent yet logically inconsistent solutions. We present \textbf{NeuroProlog}, a neurosymbolic framework that ensures verifiable reasoning by compiling math word problems into executable Prolog programs with formal verification guarantees. We propose a multi-task Cocktail training strategy that jointly optimizes three synergistic objectives in a unified symbolic representation space: (i) mathematical formula-to-rule translation (KB), (ii) natural language-to-program synthesis (SOLVE), and (iii) program-answer alignment. This joint supervision enables positive transfer, where symbolic grounding in formula translation directly improves compositional reasoning capabilities. At inference, we introduce an execution-guided decoding pipeline with fine-grained error taxonomy that enables iterative program repair and quantifies model self-debugging capacity. Comprehensive evaluation on GSM8K across four model scales (3B--32B parameters) demonstrates consistent improvements: cocktail training achieves significant accuracy gains of +5.23\% (Qwen-32B, $p < 0.01$), +3.43\% (GPT-OSS-20B, $p < 0.01$), and +5.54\% (Llama-3B, $p < 0.05$) over single-task baselines.Systematic error analysis reveals scale-dependent learning dynamics: at 32B scale, cocktail training transforms unfixable type errors (12\% repair rate) into correctable domain errors (96\% repair rate), achieving 92.7\% overall correction; at 8B scale, the same training eliminates syntactic errors but introduces semantic failures, revealing a critical capacity threshold for type-safe symbolic reasoning.

zh.title: NeuroProlog：通过鸡尾酒效应实现神经符号数学推理的多任务微调
zh.summary: NeuroProlog通过将数学应用题编译成具有形式验证保证的可执行Prolog程序，将神经符号方法与大语言模型相结合。多任务鸡尾酒训练策略联合优化公式翻译、程序合成和答案对齐，在多个模型规模上实现了相对于基线的3.4%-5.5%的准确性提高。

fr.title: NeuroProlog : Ajustement fin multi-tâches pour le raisonnement mathématique neurosymbolique via l'effet cocktail
fr.summary: NeuroProlog combine les approches neurosymboliques avec les LLM en compilant les problèmes de mots mathématiques en programmes Prolog exécutables avec des garanties de vérification formelle. Une stratégie d'entraînement multi-tâches de cocktail optimise conjointement la traduction de formules, la synthèse de programmes et l'alignement des réponses, réalisant des améliorations de précision de 3,4 % à 5,5 % par rapport aux lignes de base sur plusieurs tailles de modèle.

de.title: NeuroProlog: Multi-Task Fine-Tuning für neurosymbolisches mathematisches Denken durch den Cocktail-Effekt
de.summary: NeuroProlog kombiniert neurosymbolische Ansätze mit großen Sprachmodellen, indem es mathematische Wortaufgaben in ausführbare Prolog-Programme mit formalen Verifizierungsgarantien kompiliert. Eine Multi-Task-Cocktail-Trainingsstrategie optimiert gemeinsam Formelübersetzung, Programmsynthese und Antwortausrichtung und erreicht Genauigkeitsverbesserungen von 3,4%-5,5% gegenüber Baselines über mehrere Modellgrößen hinweg.

es.title: NeuroProlog: Ajuste fino multi-tarea para razonamiento matemático neurosimbólico a través del efecto cóctel
es.summary: NeuroProlog combina enfoques neurosimbólicos con LLM compilando problemas de palabras matemáticas en programas Prolog ejecutables con garantías de verificación formal. Una estrategia de entrenamiento de cóctel multi-tarea optimiza conjuntamente la traducción de fórmulas, la síntesis de programas y la alineación de respuestas, logrando mejoras de precisión del 3,4%-5,5% sobre las líneas base en múltiples tamaños de modelo.

title: LLM-MLFFN: Multi-Level Autonomous Driving Behavior Feature Fusion via Large Language Model
summary: LLM-MLFFN proposes an LLM-enhanced multi-level feature fusion network for classifying autonomous vehicle driving behaviors by combining numerical time-series analysis with semantic descriptions from LLMs. The framework achieves over 94% classification accuracy on the Waymo dataset, demonstrating the value of integrating structured feature modeling with language-driven reasoning.
content:
arXiv:2603.02528v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Accurate classification of autonomous vehicle (AV) driving behaviors is critical for safety validation, performance diagnosis, and traffic integration analysis. However, existing approaches primarily rely on numerical time-series modeling and often lack semantic abstraction, limiting interpretability and robustness in complex traffic environments. This paper presents LLM-MLFFN, a novel large language model (LLM)-enhanced multi-level feature fusion network designed to address the complexities of multi-dimensional driving data. The proposed LLM-MLFFN framework integrates priors from largescale pre-trained models and employs a multi-level approach to enhance classification accuracy. LLM-MLFFN comprises three core components: (1) a multi-level feature extraction module that extracts statistical, behavioral, and dynamic features to capture the quantitative aspects of driving behaviors; (2) a semantic description module that leverages LLMs to transform raw data into high-level semantic features; and (3) a dual-channel multi-level feature fusion network that combines numerical and semantic features using weighted attention mechanisms to improve robustness and prediction accuracy. Evaluation on the Waymo open trajectory dataset demonstrates the superior performance of the proposed LLM-MLFFN, achieving a classification accuracy of over 94%, surpassing existing machine learning models. Ablation studies further validate the critical contributions of multi-level fusion, feature extraction strategies, and LLM-derived semantic reasoning. These results suggest that integrating structured feature modeling with language-driven semantic abstraction provides a principled and interpretable pathway for robust autonomous driving behavior classification.

zh.title: LLM-MLFFN：通过大语言模型实现多级自动驾驶行为特征融合
zh.summary: LLM-MLFFN提出了一个LLM增强的多级特征融合网络，通过将数值时间序列分析与来自LLM的语义描述相结合来对自动驾驶车辆驾驶行为进行分类。该框架在Waymo数据集上实现了超过94%的分类准确率，展示了将结构化特征建模与语言驱动推理相整合的价值。

fr.title: LLM-MLFFN : Fusion multi-niveaux de caractéristiques de comportement de conduite autonome via modèle de langage volumineux
fr.summary: LLM-MLFFN propose un réseau de fusion de caractéristiques multi-niveaux amélioré par LLM pour classer les comportements de conduite des véhicules autonomes en combinant l'analyse des séries chronologiques numériques avec les descriptions sémantiques des LLM. Le cadre atteint une précision de classification supérieure à 94% sur l'ensemble de données Waymo, démontrant la valeur de l'intégration de la modélisation des caractéristiques structurées avec le raisonnement basé sur le langage.

de.title: LLM-MLFFN: Multi-Level-Fusion autonomer Fahrzeugverhalten-Merkmale über großes Sprachmodell
de.summary: LLM-MLFFN schlägt ein LLM-verbessertes Multi-Level-Merkmalsfusionsnetzwerk vor, um Fahrverhalten autonomer Fahrzeuge zu klassifizieren, indem numerische Zeitreihenanalyse mit semantischen Beschreibungen von LLMs kombiniert wird. Das Framework erreicht eine Klassifizierungsgenauigkeit von über 94% auf dem Waymo-Datensatz und zeigt den Wert der Integration von strukturierter Merkmalmodellierung mit sprachgesteutem Denken.

es.title: LLM-MLFFN: Fusión multi-nivel de características de comportamiento de conducción autónoma a través de modelo de lenguaje grande
es.summary: LLM-MLFFN propone una red de fusión de características multi-nivel mejorada por LLM para clasificar comportamientos de conducción de vehículos autónomos combinando análisis de series temporales numéricas con descripciones semánticas de LLM. El marco logra una precisión de clasificación superior al 94% en el conjunto de datos de Waymo, demostrando el valor de integrar modelado de características estructurado con razonamiento impulsado por lenguaje.

title: A Neuropsychologically Grounded Evaluation of LLM Cognitive Abilities
summary: The NeuroCognition benchmark evaluates LLM cognitive abilities using adapted neuropsychological tests including Raven's Progressive Matrices, spatial working memory, and cognitive flexibility tasks. While LLMs show strong text-based performance, their abilities degrade for images and complex scenarios, yet the benchmark correlates with and extends standard capability measures.
content:
arXiv:2603.02540v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit a unified "general factor" of capability across 10 benchmarks, a finding confirmed by our factor analysis of 156 models, yet they still struggle with simple, trivial tasks for humans. This is because current benchmarks focus on task completion, failing to probe the foundational cognitive abilities that highlight these behaviors. We address this by introducing the NeuroCognition benchmark, grounded in three adapted neuropsychological tests: Raven's Progressive Matrices (abstract relational reasoning), Spatial Working Memory (maintenance and systematic search), and the Wisconsin Card Sorting Test (cognitive flexibility). Our evaluation reveals that while models perform strongly on text, their performance degrades for images and with increased complexity. Furthermore, we observe that complex reasoning is not universally beneficial, whereas simple, human-like strategies yield partial gains. We also find that NeuroCognition correlates positively with standard general-capability benchmarks, while still measuring distinct cognitive abilities beyond them. Overall, NeuroCognition emphasizes where current LLMs align with human-like intelligence and where they lack core adaptive cognition, showing the potential to serve as a verifiable, scalable source for improving LLMs.

zh.title: 神经心理学基础的LLM认知能力评估
zh.summary: NeuroCognition基准通过采用改编的神经心理学测试（包括瑞文渐进矩阵、空间工作记忆和认知灵活性任务）来评估LLM的认知能力。虽然LLM在文本任务上表现强劲，但在图像和复杂场景中的能力下降，但该基准与标准能力测量相关联并扩展了它们。

fr.title: Une évaluation fondée sur la neuropsychologie des capacités cognitives des LLM
fr.summary: Le benchmark NeuroCognition évalue les capacités cognitives des LLM en utilisant des tests neuropsychologiques adaptés, notamment les matrices progressives de Raven, la mémoire de travail spatiale et les tâches de flexibilité cognitive. Bien que les LLM montrent une performance forte basée sur le texte, leurs capacités se dégradent pour les images et les scénarios complexes, mais le benchmark est corrélé avec et étend les mesures de capacité standard.

de.title: Eine neuropsychologisch fundierte Bewertung der kognitiven Fähigkeiten von LLMs
de.summary: Der NeuroCognition-Benchmark bewertet die kognitiven Fähigkeiten von LLMs durch angepasste neuropsychologische Tests, einschließlich Raven's Progressive Matrices, räumliches Arbeitsgedächtnis und Aufgaben zur kognitiven Flexibilität. Während LLMs starke textbasierte Leistungen zeigen, verschlechtern sich ihre Fähigkeiten bei Bildern und komplexen Szenarien, aber der Benchmark korreliert mit und erweitert Standardkapazitätsmessungen.

es.title: Una evaluación neuropsicológicamente fundamentada de las capacidades cognitivas de los LLM
es.summary: El benchmark NeuroCognition evalúa las capacidades cognitivas de los LLM utilizando pruebas neuropsicológicas adaptadas que incluyen Matrices Progresivas de Raven, memoria de trabajo espacial y tareas de flexibilidad cognitiva. Aunque los LLM muestran un desempeño sólido basado en texto, sus capacidades se degradan para imágenes y escenarios complejos, pero el benchmark se correlaciona con y extiende medidas de capacidad estándar.

title: AnchorDrive: LLM Scenario Rollout with Anchor-Guided Diffusion Regeneration for Safety-Critical Scenario Generation
summary: AnchorDrive combines LLMs and diffusion models for generating safety-critical scenarios in autonomous driving simulations by leveraging their complementary strengths. In the first stage, an LLM acts as a controllable driver agent; in the second stage, a diffusion model regenerates trajectories using extracted anchor points as guidance to improve realism while preserving intent.
content:
arXiv:2603.02542v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autonomous driving systems require comprehensive evaluation in safety-critical scenarios to ensure safety and robustness. However, such scenarios are rare and difficult to collect from real-world driving data, necessitating simulation-based synthesis. Yet, existing methods often exhibit limitations in both controllability and realism. From a capability perspective, LLMs excel at controllable generation guided by natural language instructions, while diffusion models are better suited for producing trajectories consistent with realistic driving distributions. Leveraging their complementary strengths, we propose AnchorDrive, a two-stage safety-critical scenario generation framework. In the first stage, we deploy an LLM as a driver agent within a closed-loop simulation, which reasons and iteratively outputs control commands under natural language constraints; a plan assessor reviews these commands and provides corrective feedback, enabling semantically controllable scenario generation. In the second stage, the LLM extracts key anchor points from the first-stage trajectories as guidance objectives, which jointly with other guidance terms steer the diffusion model to regenerate complete trajectories with improved realism while preserving user-specified intent. Experiments on the highD dataset demonstrate that AnchorDrive achieves superior overall performance in criticality, realism, and controllability, validating its effectiveness for generating controllable and realistic safety-critical scenarios.

zh.title: AnchorDrive：使用锚点引导扩散再生的LLM场景展开用于安全关键场景生成
zh.summary: AnchorDrive结合LLM和扩散模型，通过利用它们的互补优势为自动驾驶模拟生成安全关键场景。在第一阶段，LLM充当可控制的驾驶代理；在第二阶段，扩散模型使用提取的锚点作为指导来再生轨迹，以改进真实性同时保留意图。

fr.title: AnchorDrive : Déploiement de scénarios LLM avec régénération de diffusion guidée par ancrage pour la génération de scénarios critiques pour la sécurité
fr.summary: AnchorDrive combine les LLM et les modèles de diffusion pour générer des scénarios critiques pour la sécurité dans les simulations de conduite autonome en exploitant leurs forces complémentaires. Au premier stade, un LLM agit en tant qu'agent conducteur contrôlable ; au deuxième stade, un modèle de diffusion régénère les trajectoires en utilisant des points d'ancrage extraits comme guide pour améliorer le réalisme tout en préservant l'intention.

de.title: AnchorDrive: LLM-Szenario-Rollout mit ankergestützter Diffusionsregeneration für sicherheitskritische Szenarioerzeugung
de.summary: AnchorDrive kombiniert LLMs und Diffusionsmodelle, um sicherheitskritische Szenarien in autonomen Fahrsimulationen durch Nutzung ihrer komplementären Stärken zu generieren. Im ersten Stadium fungiert ein LLM als steuerbarer Fahreragent; im zweiten Stadium regeneriert ein Diffusionsmodell Trajektorien unter Verwendung extrahierter Ankerpunkte als Orientierungshilfe, um Realismus zu verbessern und gleichzeitig die Absicht zu bewahren.

es.title: AnchorDrive: Despliegue de escenarios LLM con regeneración de difusión guiada por anclaje para la generación de escenarios críticos para la seguridad
es.summary: AnchorDrive combina LLM y modelos de difusión para generar escenarios críticos para la seguridad en simulaciones de conducción autónoma aprovechando sus fortalezas complementarias. En la primera etapa, un LLM actúa como un agente conductor controlable; en la segunda etapa, un modelo de difusión regenera trayectorias utilizando puntos de anclaje extraídos como guía para mejorar el realismo mientras se preserva la intención.

title: LiveAgentBench: Comprehensive Benchmarking of Agentic Systems Across 104 Real-World Challenges
summary: LiveAgentBench introduces a comprehensive benchmark of 104 real-world AI agent scenarios sourced from social media and actual products, with 374 total tasks for validation and testing. The novel Social Perception-Driven Data Generation method ensures real-world relevance and verifiability while enabling continuous updates with fresh queries.
content:
arXiv:2603.02586v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As large language models grow more capable, general AI agents have become increasingly prevalent in practical applications. However, existing benchmarks face significant limitations, failing to represent real-world user tasks accurately. To address this gap, we present LiveAgentBench, a comprehensive benchmark with 104 scenarios that reflect real user requirements. It is constructed from publicly sourced questions on social media and real-world products. Central to our approach is the Social Perception-Driven Data Generation (SPDG) method, a novel process we developed to ensure each question's real-world relevance, task complexity, and result verifiability. We evaluate various models, frameworks, and commercial products using LiveAgentBench, revealing their practical performance and identifying areas for improvement. This release includes 374 tasks, with 125 for validation and 249 for testing. The SPDG process enables continuous updates with fresh queries from real-world interactions.

zh.title: LiveAgentBench：在104个真实世界挑战上全面基准测试代理系统
zh.summary: LiveAgentBench介绍了一个综合基准，包含来自社交媒体和实际产品的104个真实世界AI代理场景，共374个总任务用于验证和测试。新颖的社交感知驱动数据生成方法确保真实世界的相关性和可验证性，同时能够通过新鲜查询进行持续更新。

fr.title: LiveAgentBench : Benchmarking complet des systèmes d'agents sur 104 défis du monde réel
fr.summary: LiveAgentBench introduit un benchmark complet de 104 scénarios d'agents d'IA du monde réel provenant des réseaux sociaux et des produits réels, avec 374 tâches au total pour la validation et les tests. La nouvelle méthode de génération de données basée sur la perception sociale garantit la pertinence du monde réel et la vérifiabilité tout en permettant des mises à jour continues avec des requêtes fraîches.

de.title: LiveAgentBench: Umfassende Benchmarking von Agentensystemen über 104 reale Herausforderungen
de.summary: LiveAgentBench stellt einen umfassenden Benchmark von 104 realen KI-Agenten-Szenarien vor, die aus sozialen Medien und realen Produkten stammen, mit insgesamt 374 Aufgaben zur Validierung und zum Testen. Die neuartige Social Perception-Driven Data Generation Methode gewährleistet reale Relevanz und Überprüfbarkeit und ermöglicht gleichzeitig kontinuierliche Updates mit neuen Anfragen.

es.title: LiveAgentBench: Benchmarking integral de sistemas de agentes en 104 desafíos del mundo real
es.summary: LiveAgentBench introduce un benchmark integral de 104 escenarios de agentes de IA del mundo real procedentes de redes sociales y productos reales, con 374 tareas totales para validación y pruebas. El novedoso método de generación de datos impulsado por la percepción social garantiza la relevancia en el mundo real y la verificabilidad, al tiempo que permite actualizaciones continuas con consultas nuevas.
